tasks.name: N3: Top level BER simulation
tasks.owner: Gulshan Poddar, Samanway Pal, Arka Chowdhury
tasks.description:
Why: Top-level BER sim. Check the functionality of backend and front end in different gain/Bandwidth settings
Deliverable: BER results across carrier/datarates across process corner.
Risk: During simulation with process corner, we may get higher BER and we need to debug the same.
Limitation: Simulation time is 6 hours per sim.

tasks.description:
Why: Post-layout simulation of the serializer (@125M to 1G)
Deliverable: Timing alignment (set up/hold) of all the stages
Risk: NA
Limitation: We can't go beyond 4GHz.
Result: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/p/shovan/IQAY4DO5aKrgTJQsWRxcjsP3Ad5D\_QbAOr1Kn6DNuiRokuI?e=dnHvNM](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/p/shovan/IQAY4DO5aKrgTJQsWRxcjsP3Ad5D_QbAOr1Kn6DNuiRokuI?e=dnHvNM)
Post Layout Sims are from Slide No 20

Summary:
This Serializer will take 8 bit Low Speed Data Input & 4 Clk Input So that It can give 1 bit Fast Data Output.
Output Freq.= 8\*Input Freq.

tasks.name: N3: 2 device wrapper step by step debug
tasks.description:
This task is being logged in the following guide: FPGA Emulation: 2 Device on a single FPGA ([https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-45897/8cqezuv-87457](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-45897/8cqezuv-87457))
### April 3, 2026
Managed to synthesize a close to full 2 device bitstream including the analog, core, and digital blocks and conducted the following tests:
*   Device 1 Core-mode ![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/972fc90c-1668-407e-819a-a4bef7b2e0f2/image.png)
*   Device 1 Core-bypass mode ![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/c9d4c178-06bd-4bcb-85fc-f9feb9711cdb/image.png)
*   Device 2 Core-mode (Debugging...)
*   Device 2 Core-bypass mode ![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/eb67e8e0-3308-464f-b616-9574bd47a2c0/image.png)
**Bitstream + Implementation Checkpoints and Logs**

[full\_2devices\_adapter\_board.zip](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/d92d7867-5e2d-412e-8b83-585c89181b6b/full_2devices_adapter_board.zip)

### April 06, 2026
There is an issue with the Cortex-M0 for the **second device**
*   I am able to attach over SWD but from inspecting the SRAM using the JMem memory viewer, the SRAM isn't being flashed properly
*   I wasn't able to run neither the `wir_fifo_readback` application nor some of the core peripheral tests like the `dma` test
*   I scaled back the design once again to an implementation where only 2 cores are implemented
    *   I was able to then validate a valid region for the second device by running the `dma` test, checking the results with the debugger and the UART prints
*   Knowing the valid region, I defined a Pblock range for the placement of device 2 and added the WIR components again
    *   In the most recent bitstream, I generated a Full Single Dev 1 + Digital and core Dev 2
        *   Passed Device 1 readback tests in both core and core-bypass modes
        *   I was able to program Device 2's SRAM this time and passed the `dma` test
        *   With this I flashed the `wir_fifo_readback` test in the second device (a milestone that was not possible in the attached bitstream above)
        *   In both core and core-bypass modes, the readback test failed in the config read/write step
